_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about how our daily lives are similar to and different from the daily lives of people in the past.

_source.w: We are learning about how our daily lives are similar to and different from the daily lives of people in the past.
y: Comparing our lives with the past helps us see how much has changed.
Noticing what stayed the same reminds us that some things always matter — like play, food, and friends.
Understanding daily life in the past helps us appreciate what we have today.
eg: Comparing old school photos with what our classroom looks like now.
Looking at toys from long ago and comparing them with toys we play with today.
Asking a grandparent what games they played at lunchtime when they were at school.
Comparing a lunchbox from 50 years ago with a lunchbox today.
Looking at old and new photos of the school playground and discussing what changed.
Acting out a day in the life of a child from the past.
